name: implicit (Fast Implicit Collaborative Filtering for Python)

performance.notes: Highly optimized C++/CUDA; GPU-accelerated ALS; inference <1ms for top-N

agent_readiness.notes: Fast ALS matrix factorization for implicit feedback. import implicit. from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix. model = implicit.als.AlternatingLeastSquares(factors=50, iterations=20). model.fit(user_item_matrix). ids, scores = model.recommend(userid=0, user_items=user_item_matrix[0], N=10). model.similar_items(itemid=100, N=10). Sparse matrix input (scipy csr_matrix). GPU acceleration via CUDA. Models: ALS, BPR, LMF, BM25. GPU: implicit.gpu.als.AlternatingLeastSquares(). Saves/loads: model.save('model.npz'). Used in production recommendation systems.

gotchas: Input must be scipy sparse matrix (user x item) — data prep required
Implicit feedback only (purchase/click counts, not ratings)
GPU version requires CUDA toolkit matching PyTorch version
No built-in evaluation metrics — must compute MAP/NDCG separately
best_when: E-commerce recommendations from implicit purchase/click data
Collaborative filtering at scale (millions of users/items)
When ALS/BPR matrix factorization is the algorithm of choice
Production recommendation systems needing fast GPU-accelerated training
avoid_when: Explicit ratings (use SVD/SVD++ from Surprise instead)
Small datasets where simple popularity baseline suffices
summary: implicit provides fast GPU-accelerated collaborative filtering for implicit feedback. AlternatingLeastSquares(factors=50).fit(csr_matrix). model.recommend(userid, user_items, N=10). model.similar_items(itemid, N=10). Models: ALS, BPR, LMF, BM25. GPU via CUDA. Input: scipy sparse user-item matrix. MIT license. Production-grade — used in large-scale recommendation systems for e-commerce and media. Best for implicit feedback (clicks/purchases) at scale.
